Package jsim.api

Class SimWorld

java.lang.Object
jsim.api.SimWorld
public final class SimWorld extends Object
Top-level public API for the JSim physics simulation.

Create a world, add bodies, and call step() each robot loop iteration: 


 SimWorld world = new SimWorld();

 // Static floor
 world.addBody(new SimBodyBuilder("Floor")
     .planeCollider(0, 0, 1, 0)
     .material(Material.CARPET));

 // Game piece
 SimBody ball = world.addBody(new SimBodyBuilder("Ball")
     .position(1, 2, 0.1)
     .mass(0.24)
     .sphereCollider(0.085)
     .material(Material.RUBBER));

 // In your simulationPeriodic():
 world.step();
 Pose3d pose = ball.getPose();

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    SimWorld()
    Creates a world with the default timestep and solver iteration count.
    SimWorld(double fixedTimestepSeconds)
    Creates a world with a custom fixed timestep and the default solver iteration count.
    SimWorld(double fixedTimestepSeconds, int solverIterations)
    Creates a world with a custom fixed timestep and solver iteration count.

    Description

    Build a body from the supplied builder, register it in the world, and return the high-level SimBody handle.

    Register a custom force generator (drag, magnus, spring, custom motor model, etc.).

    Create and register a MagnusForce for the given spinning body.
    SimBody
    findBody(String name)
    Find the first body with the given name, or null if none.
    SimBody
    findRobot(RobotId id)
    Find the robot body registered with the given alliance-station identity, or null if no body was added with that RobotId.
    List<SimBody>
    getBodies()
    Read-only list of all registered bodies in insertion order.

    The fixed timestep this world was configured with (seconds).

    Unregister a previously added force generator.

    Advance the simulation by the fixed timestep configured at construction time.

    Advance by an explicit timestep (useful for tests or variable-rate callers).

  • Constructor Details

    • SimWorld

      public SimWorld()
      Creates a world with the default timestep and solver iteration count.

    • SimWorld

      public SimWorld(double fixedTimestepSeconds)
      Creates a world with a custom fixed timestep and the default solver iteration count.
      Parameters:
      fixedTimestepSeconds - the physics timestep in seconds

    • SimWorld

      public SimWorld(double fixedTimestepSeconds, int solverIterations)
      Creates a world with a custom fixed timestep and solver iteration count.
      Parameters:
      fixedTimestepSeconds - the physics timestep in seconds
      solverIterations - number of constraint-solver iterations per step

  • Method Details

    • addBody

      public SimBody addBody(SimBodyBuilder builder)
      Build a body from the supplied builder, register it in the world, and return the high-level SimBody handle.
      Parameters:
      builder - configured body builder
      Returns:
      the newly created SimBody

    • removeBody

      public void removeBody(SimBody sb)
      Remove a previously added body from the simulation.
      Parameters:
      sb - the body to remove

    • getBodies

      public List<SimBody> getBodies()
      Read-only list of all registered bodies in insertion order.
      Returns:
      unmodifiable list of all bodies

    • addForceGenerator

      public void addForceGenerator(ForceGenerator fg)
      Register a custom force generator (drag, magnus, spring, custom motor model, etc.). The generator is called every tick after built-in gravity.
      Parameters:
      fg - the force generator to register

    • removeForceGenerator

      public void removeForceGenerator(ForceGenerator fg)
      Unregister a previously added force generator.
      Parameters:
      fg - the force generator to remove

    • addDragForce

      public DragForce addDragForce(SimBody body, double linearDamping, double angularDamping)
      Create and register a DragForce for the given body.

      Example usage: 

      
 DragForce drag = world.addDragForce(ball, 0.1, 0.05);
 // later, to remove:
 world.removeForceGenerator(drag);
      Parameters:
      body - the body to apply drag to
      linearDamping - linear drag coefficient (N·s/m)
      angularDamping - rotational drag coefficient (N·m·s/rad)
      Returns:
      the registered DragForce

    • addMagnusForce

      public MagnusForce addMagnusForce(SimBody body, double magnusCoefficient)
      Create and register a MagnusForce for the given spinning body.

      The Magnus effect produces a lift force perpendicular to both the spin axis and the velocity vector, causing curved flight for spinning game pieces.

      Parameters:
      body - the spinning body to apply Magnus lift to
      magnusCoefficient - Magnus lift coefficient k (kg); typically 0.1–2.0 for FRC game pieces
      Returns:
      the registered MagnusForce

    • step

      public void step()
      Advance the simulation by the fixed timestep configured at construction time. Call once per robot loop iteration from simulationPeriodic().

    • step

      public void step(double dtSeconds)
      Advance by an explicit timestep (useful for tests or variable-rate callers). Determinism is only guaranteed when dt is the same every call.
      Parameters:
      dtSeconds - timestep in seconds

    • getTimestep

      public double getTimestep()
      The fixed timestep this world was configured with (seconds).
      Returns:
      timestep in seconds

    • findBody

      public SimBody findBody(String name)
      Find the first body with the given name, or null if none.
      Parameters:
      name - the body name to search for
      Returns:
      the matching body, or null

    • findRobot

      public SimBody findRobot(RobotId id)
      Find the robot body registered with the given alliance-station identity, or null if no body was added with that RobotId.
      Parameters:
      id - the alliance-station identity to look up
      Returns:
      the matching robot body, or null
